diagrams.title: Simple Factory — Class Diagram
diagrams.description: Clients call Factory.create(type). Factory returns the right Notification subclass.

steps.label: Product Interface
steps.description: The common contract all created objects share
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class Notification {
        <<interface>>
        +send(message: String) void
        +validate() boolean
    }
    note for Notification "The common contract\nall products share"

steps.label: + Concrete Products
steps.description: Each notification type implements the interface

steps.label: + Factory
steps.description: The Factory class encapsulates creation logic

steps.label: + Client
steps.description: Client only knows Factory + Interface, never concrete classes

diagrams.title: Factory Method — Class Diagram
diagrams.description: Abstract creator defines the skeleton. Subclasses override the factory method to decide WHAT to create.

steps.label: Abstract Creator
steps.description: Defines the template with a deferred factory method
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class NotificationService {
        <<abstract>>
        #createNotification()* Notification
        +notifyUser(msg: String) void
    }
    note for NotificationService "Template Method calls\ncreateNotification()\nSubclasses decide WHAT"

steps.label: + Concrete Creators
steps.description: Each subclass overrides createNotification() with its own product

steps.label: + Products
steps.description: Complete picture: creators produce matching products

diagrams.title: Abstract Factory — Class Diagram
diagrams.description: Creates FAMILIES of related objects. Regional compliance: tax + receipt + currency must vary TOGETHER.

steps.label: Product Families
steps.description: Three product interfaces that form a family

steps.label: + Abstract Factory
steps.description: One factory creates the entire family

steps.label: + Concrete Families
steps.description: India factory creates Indian family, US factory creates US family
steps.diagram:
classDiagram
    class RegionalComplianceFactory {
        <<interface>>
        +createTaxCalculator() TaxCalculator
        +createReceiptGenerator() ReceiptGenerator
        +createCurrencyFormatter() CurrencyFormatter
    }
    class IndiaComplianceFactory {
        +createTaxCalculator() TaxCalculator
        +createReceiptGenerator() ReceiptGenerator
        +createCurrencyFormatter() CurrencyFormatter
    }
    class USComplianceFactory {
        +createTaxCalculator() TaxCalculator
        +createReceiptGenerator() ReceiptGenerator
        +createCurrencyFormatter() CurrencyFormatter
    }
    RegionalComplianceFactory <|.. IndiaComplianceFactory
    RegionalComplianceFactory <|.. USComplianceFactory
    IndiaComplianceFactory ..> GSTCalculator : creates
    IndiaComplianceFactory ..> IndiaReceiptGenerator : creates
    IndiaComplianceFactory ..> RupeeFormatter : creates
    USComplianceFactory ..> USTaxCalculator : creates
    USComplianceFactory ..> USReceiptGenerator : creates
    USComplianceFactory ..> DollarFormatter : creates

diagrams.title: Factory Flow — Sequence Diagram
diagrams.description: What happens at runtime when OrderService needs a notification

steps.label: Full Flow
steps.description: Client → Factory → Product — the runtime sequence
steps.diagram:
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as OrderService
    participant F as NotificationFactory
    participant N as EmailNotification

    C->>F: create("email")
    activate F
    F->>F: lookup registry
    F->>N: new EmailNotification()
    activate N
    N-->>F: instance
    deactivate N
    F-->>C: Notification (interface ref)
    deactivate F
    C->>N: send("Order confirmed")
    activate N
    N->>N: validate()
    N->>N: dispatch via SMTP
    N-->>C: success
    deactivate N
    Note over C: Client never knew it was Email.\nSwap to SMS = change Factory config only.
